response.justification: The candidate brings genuine, sustained geospatial experience (a decade building an open-source GIS/mapping platform used by Green Map, plus a FOSS4G talk) and real systems-programming depth from low-latency DLang microservices handling millions of requests per second at Sociomantic. However, the role's primary requirement is modern C++ or Rust in a commercial setting, and his only professional C++ work dates to 2014-2015 at National Instruments; his recent decade is dominated by TypeScript/Node full-stack product work. He also has no routing, navigation guidance, or map-tile pipeline experience specifically, so he would be learning the core domain and re-ramping on the core language simultaneously.
strengths: Long-running hands-on geospatial/GIS platform work (GISCollective, Green Map) directly relevant to map data processing
Low-latency systems experience with async I/O, caching, and fibers in DLang at very high request volumes
Strong engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling author) matching the role's quality-assurance emphasis
concerns: Modern C++ experience is stale (last professional use ~2015) and no Rust experience is evident
No navigation, routing, or tile-generation pipeline experience despite adjacent GIS background
Recent roles are web/full-stack product work, not large-scale data pipeline or distributed infrastructure ownership

key_factors.factor: Stale C++ / no Rust vs. primary language requirement
